description: Blackstonia perfoliata or more commonly known as the yellow-wort is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae found around the Mediterranean Basin, but extending into northwestern Europe.

description: Centaurium erythraea is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family known by the names common centaury and European centaury. It is also commonly known as feverfoullie, gentian or centaury.

care.tips: Native to: BGM BE, IRE NI, INI, AUSTRALASIA, RHO OO

description: Centaurium littorale, the seaside centaury, is a species of flowering plant belonging to the family Gentianaceae.

care.tips: Native to: YUG CR, BGM BE, POR OO, FIN, IRE NI
Native to: AZO OO, BAL OO, ALG, COR, CNY OO

description: Centaurium pulchellum  is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family known by the common name lesser centaury, or slender centaury. It differs from Centaurium erythraea by lacking basal rosette of leaves and by having a developed peduncle below the flowers. It is often much smaller, less than ten centimetres. It is native to the southern temperate parts of Europe.

description: Exaculum is a monotypic genus of flowering plants belonging to the family Gentianaceae. The only species is Exaculum pusillum.
Pods form after the flower fades. They should be allowed to dry on the plant.  Once dried, the pods can be opened to collect the seeds.  Seeds are best sown in the fall when fresh.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Similar plants: Betonica officinalis

description: Gentiana andrewsii, the bottle gentian, closed gentian, or closed bottle gentian, is an herbaceous species of flowering plant in the gentian family Gentianaceae. Gentiana andrewsii is native to northeastern North America, from the Dakotas to the East Coast and through eastern Canada.

Gentiana asclepiadea, the willow gentian, is a species of flowering plant of the genus Gentiana in the family Gentianaceae, native to central and eastern Europe. It occurs primarily in mountain (montane) woodland, though it may be found in less wooded open pasture in some areas, perhaps persisting after woodland clearance.
Gentiana cruciata, the star gentian or cross gentian, is a herbaceous perennial flowering plant in the Gentianaceae family.

Gentiana lutea, the great yellow gentian, is a species of gentian native to the mountains of central and southern Europe, including the Alps, the Carpathians, the Pyrenees, the Apennines, and the mountains of the Balkan Peninsula. It is the type species of the genus Gentiana.
Gentiana nivalis, the snow gentian or Alpine gentian, is a species of the genus Gentiana. It grows to a height of 3–15 centimetres (1.2–5.9 in).

Gentiana pneumonanthe, the marsh gentian, is a species of the genus Gentiana. It was the first wildflower announced as flower of the year in Germany in 1980. The species can be found in marshes and moorlands. It is the host-plant of the Alcon blue .
Capsules split into 2 sections, releasing numerous small seeds that can be dispersed by wind or water.

care.light_guide: NC State: Partial Shade (Direct sunlight only part of the day, 2-6 hours)
care.tips: Similar plants: Coreopsis gladiata
common_problems: Root rot can occur in poorly drained soil.

description: Gentiana verna, the spring gentian, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, and one of its smallest members, normally only growing to a height of a few centimetres.

Gentianella amarella, the autumn gentian, autumn dwarf gentian, or autumn felwort, is a short biennial plant flowering plant in the gentian family, Gentianaceae. It is found throughout Northern Europe, the western and northern United States, and Canada.
Gentianella campestris, common name field gentian, is a small herbaceous biennial flowering plant in the Gentianaceae native to Europe. Its bluish-purple flowers contain four petals.
Capsule divides into 2 parts to release the numerous tiny seeds. These seeds are distributed by wind and water.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.tips: Similar plants: Anacis major

description: Gentianopsis is a genus of flowering plants in the gentian family known commonly as fringed gentians. These are similar to the gentians of genus Gentiana. Most have flowers which are blue to purple in color. They may be annual or perennial. They are native to Eurasia and temperate North America.
